title: Getting Started
description: Set up your local environment and run the dev server

blocks.text: This site is a Howl app. The only runtime you need is Deno 2.x.
From the project root, install the cached deps:
Copy the example env file and edit values to match your setup.

rows: Title shown in the brand bar and HTML <title>
Port the dev server binds to
Hostname the dev server binds to

blocks.text: Open http://localhost:8000. Edits to client/ and server/ hot-reload.
Each doc is a JSON file under server/docs/. Three steps to add one:

items: Drop a new JSON file: server/docs/my-topic.json
Append a manifest entry to server/docs/manifest.json with a unique slug + order
Register the import in server/docs/reader.ts (see the commented examples there)

blocks.text: The slug in the JSON file MUST match the manifest slug and the registry key. Slugs are URL-safe a-z0-9-.
Each doc has sections, and each section has blocks. Available block types:

items: { type: 'h3', text }    — sub-heading inside a section
{ type: 'code', lang, text, filename? } — fenced code block, syntax-highlighted for ts/tsx/js/jsx
{ type: 'table', headers: string[], rows: string[][] } — table

blocks.text: See client/pages/docs/[slug].tsx for the renderer if you want to add a new block type.

deno task build
deno task start
Or compile to a single binary:
deno task build && deno task compile
